behaviors.description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings (code fences, backticks, $vars) parse successfully and proceed through normal hook input handling
behaviors.justification: The behavior includes 'proceed through normal hook input handling', meaning more than just JSON parsing logic. While a Unit test of the parser alone might catch obvious bugs (e.g., parser rejects backticks), it would miss failures that occur during integration with the full input pipeline or subprocess stdin communication. A pure in-process JSON parse test could hide encoding issues that only manifest when markdown is transmitted via subprocess stdin. Integration testing wiring the full input handler (with mocked I/O) would catch most bugs; System testing is needed only if the failure is specific to subprocess communication.
behaviors.test_description: Valid JSON payloads containing markdown-heavy strings (code fences \`\`\`, backticks in strings, $variable syntax, escaped quotes) flow through the full hook input handling pipeline and complete normally without parse errors, premature stream termination, or handler crashes
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description matches Integration level: verifies the full pipeline (not just parsing) with sufficient setup to catch integration wiring bugs, but doesn't require real subprocess communication

behaviors.description: Truly malformed JSON payloads produce a `json_parse_failed` trace record with enough context to diagnose the malformed section
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires verifying that trace records are produced AND contain diagnostic context (not just that the parser detects an error). A Unit test mocking the trace call would verify 'was trace attempted', but wouldn't catch bugs where the trace lacks context or diagnostic info. Integration testing that wires the parser to the trace system and inspects the actual trace content is the minimum. System testing (writing to real trace file) adds confidence about I/O reliability but Integration with trace capture is sufficient to catch the behavior bug.
behaviors.test_description: When malformed JSON is supplied (e.g., unclosed brace, trailing comma, invalid escape sequence, missing quotes), a json_parse_failed trace record is created with diagnostic context: character position, character excerpt surrounding the error, and error message sufficient to identify the malformed section without needing to re-parse the input
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description verifies actual trace content (not just that code attempts to trace), matching the Integration requirement

behaviors.description: On parse failure, the hook exits in a non-crashing way while still recording the failure signal for later analysis
behaviors.justification: The phrase 'hook exits in a non-crashing way' is fundamentally about OS-level process behavior. Exit codes, signals, and crash handling cannot be tested at Unit or Integration level — they require actual subprocess invocation. A Unit test of exception handling or an Integration test of error logic could pass while the hook still crashes with SIGSEGV or exits with the wrong code when run as a real subprocess. System testing that invokes the hook subprocess and verifies exit status is the minimum to catch the real failure.
behaviors.test_description: When the hook subprocess receives malformed JSON on stdin, it exits with a non-zero exit code (indicating error), is not killed by a signal (SIGSEGV, SIGABRT), and does not dump core. The failure trace is recorded before exit, so later analysis tools have diagnostic data
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description explicitly tests subprocess exit behavior, matching System level requirement

behaviors.description: A real hook invocation path (stdin -> parser -> trace sink) produces the expected trace artifact when malformed input is supplied
behaviors.justification: This behavior explicitly specifies 'real hook invocation path' and the full chain stdin -> parser -> trace sink. By definition, it requires System-level testing: real subprocess invocation, real stdin communication, real trace sink I/O. There is no lower level that satisfies the stated requirement of testing the real invocation path.
behaviors.test_description: End-to-end System test: invoke the real hook subprocess, provide malformed JSON on stdin, verify the trace artifact is written to the configured trace sink (file, syslog, or structured log), and verify it contains a json_parse_failed event with sufficient diagnostic context to identify the malformed section
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description is explicit about 'real' invocation and trace sink, matching System requirement exactly

behaviors.description: Regression tests distinguish malformed-JSON failures from valid-markdown payloads, preventing false attribution to markdown content itself
behaviors.justification: This behavior requires running two distinct scenarios and verifying they produce different outcomes. A Unit test of logic alone (e.g., 'if json parses, continue; if not, trace') might pass while the actual system confuses the two cases. Integration testing that runs both the valid-markdown and truly-malformed cases through the pipeline and verifies different trace outcomes are produced is the minimum. This catches bugs where valid markdown falsely triggers error traces or where malformed JSON doesn't trigger them.
behaviors.test_description: Regression test suite includes paired test cases: (a) valid JSON with markdown-heavy content (code fences, backticks, escaped quotes, variable syntax) must proceed normally with no parse-failed traces, and (b) truly malformed JSON (unclosed braces, syntax errors) must produce json_parse_failed traces. Verify that case (a) does not produce false parse-failed traces and case (b) always produces them, preventing regression where markdown syntax is confused with malformed JSON
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test description runs both scenarios through the pipeline and verifies different outcomes, matching Integration requirement; Unit-only logic testing would not catch behavioral bugs
